title: Apply Format to Data (like 'SAS' PUT function) — fput
text: x Vector values format format ks_format object character string naming format global format library. ... Additional arguments expression labels. Positional arguments mapped .x1, .x2, etc. inside expression labels. Can vectors length x scalars (recycled). keep_na Logical. TRUE, preserve NA output instead applying missing label.

title: Apply Format to Data (like 'SAS' PUT function) — fput
text: Character vector formatted labels

title: Apply Format to Data (like 'SAS' PUT function) — fput
text: function handles missing values following order: NA, NULL, NaN -> Uses format's missing_label defined Exact matches -> Uses defined value-label mapping Range matches (numeric) -> Uses range label match -> Uses format's other_label returns original value Expression labels: label string contains .x1, .x2, etc., evaluated R expression apply-time. Extra data passed positional arguments: Numeric patterns: numeric format created single unnamed pattern string (example fnew("$%,.2f", type = "numeric")), fput/fputn apply pattern numeric values directly, including grouping marks literal prefix/suffix text. Case-insensitive matching: format ignore_case = TRUE, key matching case-insensitive character formats.
code: stat_fmt <- fnew("n" = "sprintf('%s', .x1)",                  "pct" = "sprintf('%.1f%%', .x1 * 100)") fput(c("n", "pct"), stat_fmt, c(42, 0.15)) # Returns: "42" "15.0%"

title: Apply Format to Data (like 'SAS' PUT function) — fput
code: # Basic discrete formatting fnew("M" = "Male", "F" = "Female", .missing = "Unknown", name = "sex") fput(c("M", "F", NA, "X"), "sex") #> [1] "Male"    "Female"  "Unknown" "X"       # [1] "Male" "Female" "Unknown" "X"  # Preserve NA instead of applying missing label sex_f <- fnew("M" = "Male", "F" = "Female", .missing = "Unknown") fput(c("M", "F", NA), sex_f, keep_na = TRUE) #> [1] "Male"   "Female" NA       # [1] "Male" "Female" NA  # Numeric range formatting fparse(text = ' VALUE score (numeric)   (0, 50]  = "Low"   (50, 100] = "High"   .other   = "Out of range" ; ') fput(c(0, 1, 50, 51, 100, 101), "score") #> [1] "Out of range" "Low"          "Low"          "High"         "High"         #> [6] "Out of range" # [1] "Out of range" "Low" "Low" "High" "High" "Out of range" fclear() #> All formats cleared from library.

title: Apply Format and Return All Matches (Multilabel) — fput_all
code: # Basic multilabel: a value can match multiple labels age_ml <- fnew(   "0,5,TRUE,TRUE" = "Infant",   "6,11,TRUE,TRUE" = "Child",   "12,17,TRUE,TRUE" = "Teen",   "0,17,TRUE,TRUE" = "Minor",   "18,64,TRUE,TRUE" = "Adult",   "65,Inf,TRUE,TRUE" = "Senior",   name = "age_ml", type = "numeric", multilabel = TRUE )  fput_all(c(3, 15, 25), age_ml) #> [[1]] #> [1] "Infant" "Minor"  #>  #> [[2]] #> [1] "Minor" "Teen"  #>  #> [[3]] #> [1] "Adult" #>  # [[1]] "Infant" "Minor" # [[2]] "Teen" "Minor" # [[3]] "Adult"  # Multilabel with .missing and .other fnew(   "0,100,TRUE,TRUE"  = "Valid Score",   "0,49,TRUE,TRUE"   = "Below Average",   "50,100,TRUE,TRUE" = "Above Average",   "90,100,TRUE,TRUE" = "Excellent",   .missing = "No Score",   .other = "Out of Range",   name = "score_ml", type = "numeric", multilabel = TRUE ) fput_all(c(95, 45, NA, 150), "score_ml") #> [[1]] #> [1] "Valid Score"   "Above Average" "Excellent"     #>  #> [[2]] #> [1] "Below Average" "Valid Score"   #>  #> [[3]] #> [1] "No Score" #>  #> [[4]] #> [1] "Out of Range" #>  # [[1]] "Valid Score" "Above Average" "Excellent" # [[2]] "Valid Score" "Below Average" # [[3]] "No Score" # [[4]] "Out of Range"  # Parse multilabel from text fparse(text = ' VALUE risk (numeric, multilabel)   [0, 3]  = "Low Risk"   [0, 7]  = "Monitored"   (3, 7]  = "Medium Risk"   (7, 10] = "High Risk" ; ') fput_all(c(2, 5, 9), "risk") #> [[1]] #> [1] "Low Risk"  "Monitored" #>  #> [[2]] #> [1] "Monitored"   "Medium Risk" #>  #> [[3]] #> [1] "High Risk" #>  # [[1]] "Low Risk" "Monitored" # [[2]] "Monitored" "Medium Risk" # [[3]] "High Risk" fclear() #> All formats cleared from library.

title: Apply Format to Data Frame Columns — fput_df
code: # Apply formats to multiple columns df <- data.frame(   id = 1:6,   sex = c("M", "F", "M", "F", NA, "X"),   age = c(15, 25, 45, 70, 35, NA),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  sex_f <- fnew("M" = "Male", "F" = "Female", .missing = "Unknown") fparse(text = ' VALUE age (numeric)   [0, 18)   = "Child"   [18, 65)  = "Adult"   [65, HIGH] = "Senior"   .missing  = "Age Unknown" ; ') age_f <- format_get("age")  fput_df(df, sex = sex_f, age = age_f, suffix = "_label") #>   id  sex age sex_label   age_label #> 1  1    M  15      Male       Child #> 2  2    F  25    Female       Adult #> 3  3    M  45      Male       Adult #> 4  4    F  70    Female      Senior #> 5  5 <NA>  35   Unknown       Adult #> 6  6    X  NA         X Age Unknown  # Date formatting in data frames patients <- data.frame(   id = 1:4,   visit_date = as.Date(c("2025-01-10", "2025-02-15", "2025-03-20", NA)),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) visit_fmt <- fnew_date("DATE9.", name = "visit_fmt", .missing = "NOT RECORDED") fput_df(patients, visit_date = visit_fmt) #>   id visit_date visit_date_fmt #> 1  1 2025-01-10      10JAN2025 #> 2  2 2025-02-15      15FEB2025 #> 3  3 2025-03-20      20MAR2025 #> 4  4       <NA>   NOT RECORDED fclear() #> All formats cleared from library.

what: Apply Format Using a Composite Key — fputk
title: Apply Format Using a Composite Key — fputk
text: Convenience wrapper around [fput()] pastes multiple vectors together composite key lookup. Useful format keyed combination several columns (e.g., `USUBJID|VISITNUM`).

title: Apply Format Using a Composite Key — fputk
code: fputk(..., format, sep = "|", keep_na = FALSE, na_as_string = FALSE)

title: Apply Format Using a Composite Key — fputk
text: ... Vectors paste together composite key. vectors recycled common length [paste()]. format [ks_format] object registered format name (character string). sep Separator inserted pasted components (default `"|"`). keep_na `TRUE`, `NA` inputs remain `NA` output instead mapped via `.missing`. Passed [fput()]. na_as_string `FALSE` (default), `NA` component propagates composite key (restored `NA_character_` [paste()] step) [fput()] can apply `.missing` handling. `TRUE`, literal string `"NA"` produced [paste()] kept, useful format built composite keys via `fmap(paste(..., sep = "|"), values)` — [paste()] converts `NA` `"NA"` sides, round-trip lookup matches.

title: Apply Format Using a Composite Key — fputk
text: character vector formatted labels, length   (recycled) input vectors.

title: Apply Format Using a Composite Key — fputk
code: # Build a lookup keyed on two columns fnew(   "A|1" = "2025-01-15",   "A|2" = "2025-02-20",   "B|1" = "2025-03-10",   .other = "NOT FOUND",   name = "visit_date",   type = "character" )  subj  <- c("A", "A", "B", "B") visit <- c(1, 2, 1, 3)  fputk(subj, visit, format = "visit_date") #> [1] "2025-01-15" "2025-02-20" "2025-03-10" "NOT FOUND"  # -> "2025-01-15" "2025-02-20" "2025-03-10" "NOT FOUND"  fclear() #> All formats cleared from library.  # Composite key with NA components matching a paste()-built format fnew(   fmap(     paste(c("CHEM", "COAG"), c("ALB", "INR"), c("g/L", NA), sep = "|"),     c("ALB", "INR")   ),   name = "lb_param", type = "character" )  fputk(c("CHEM", "COAG"), c("ALB", "INR"), c("g/L", NA),       format = "lb_param", na_as_string = TRUE) #> [1] "ALB" "INR" # -> "ALB" "INR"  fclear() #> All formats cleared from library.

title: ksformat: 'SAS'-Style 'PROC FORMAT' for R — ksformat-package
text: Provides 'SAS' 'PROC FORMAT'-like functionality creating applying value formats R. package supports mapping values labels, range-based formatting, reverse formatting (invalue), date/time/datetime formatting, proper handling missing values (NA, NULL, NaN).

title: ksformat: 'SAS'-Style 'PROC FORMAT' for R — ksformat-package
text: Format creation: fnew — create value--label mappings (formats) finput — create reverse mappings (label--value invalues) fnew_bid — create format invalue simultaneously fnew_date — create date/time/datetime formats ('SAS'-style     custom strftime patterns) fparse — parse 'SAS'-like format definitions text file fimport — import formats 'SAS' CNTLOUT CSV file e — mark label expression evaluation apply-time Format application: fput — apply format vector (value label) fputn — apply numeric format name (like 'SAS' PUTN) fputc — apply character format name (like 'SAS' PUTC) fput_all — apply multilabel format returning matching labels fput_df — apply formats data frame columns Reverse formatting: finputn — apply numeric invalue name (like 'SAS' INPUTN) finputc — apply character invalue name (like 'SAS' INPUTC) Format library: format_get — retrieve format global library fprint — list display registered formats fclear — remove one formats library format_library_app — open interactive library browser (Shiny) fexport — export formats 'SAS'-like text Utilities: is_missing — check NA, NaN, empty strings range_spec — create range specification object Key features: Discrete range-based numeric formatting configurable     inclusive/exclusive bounds Multilabel formats — value can match multiple labels     (multilabel = TRUE fnew, retrieved     fput_all) Case-insensitive matching (ignore_case = TRUE     fnew) Expression labels — labels containing .x1, .x2,     etc. evaluated apply-time; see also e Date/time/datetime formatting built-'SAS' format     names (auto-resolved) custom strftime patterns Global format library auto-registration     case-insensitive name lookup CNTLOUT import — read format catalogues exported 'SAS' Cheat sheet: run ksformat_cheatsheet() open HTML version browser, see files system.file("doc", package = "ksformat").

title: ksformat: 'SAS'-Style 'PROC FORMAT' for R — ksformat-package
text: Maintainer: Vladimir Larchenko vladimir.larchenko@keystatsolutions.com Authors: Vladimir Larchenko vladimir.larchenko@keystatsolutions.com Igor Aleschenkov igor.aleschenkov@keystatsolutions.com

title: ksformat: 'SAS'-Style 'PROC FORMAT' for R — ksformat-package
code: # Discrete format fnew("M" = "Male", "F" = "Female", .missing = "Unknown", name = "sex") fput(c("M", "F", NA), "sex") #> [1] "Male"    "Female"  "Unknown"  # Numeric range format (parsed from text) fparse(text = ' VALUE age (numeric)   [0, 18)  = "Child"   [18, 65) = "Adult" ; ') fputn(c(5, 25), "age") #> [1] "Child" "Adult"  # Bidirectional format + invalue fnew_bid("A" = "Active", "I" = "Inactive", name = "status") #> $format #> KS Format:status #> Type: character  #> Mappings: #>   A => Active #>   I => Inactive #>  #> $invalue #> KS Invalue: status_inv #> Target Type: character  #> Mappings: #>   Active => A #>   Inactive => I #>  fputc("A", "status") #> [1] "Active" finputc("Active", "status_inv") #> [1] "A"  # Multilabel format ml <- fnew(   "0,17,TRUE,TRUE" = "Pediatric",   "18,Inf,TRUE,TRUE" = "Adult",   "0,Inf,TRUE,TRUE" = "Any Age",   name = "agegrp", type = "numeric", multilabel = TRUE ) fput_all(c(10, 30), ml) #> [[1]] #> [1] "Pediatric" "Any Age"   #>  #> [[2]] #> [1] "Any Age" "Adult"   #>   # Date format (SAS-style, auto-resolved) fputn(Sys.Date(), "DATE9.") #> [1] "09JUL2026"  # Export and library management cat(fexport(sex = format_get("sex"))) #> VALUE sex (character) #>   "M" = "Male" #>   "F" = "Female" #>   .missing = "Unknown" #> ; flist()           # character vector of registered names #> [1] "DATE9."     "age"        "agegrp"     "sex"        "status"     #> [6] "status_inv" fprint() #> Registered formats: #>   DATE9. - VALUE (date, DATE9.), 0 mapping(s) #>   age - VALUE (numeric), 2 mapping(s) #>   agegrp - VALUE (numeric), 3 mapping(s) #>   sex - VALUE (character), 2 mapping(s) #>   status - VALUE (character), 2 mapping(s) #>   status_inv - INVALUE (character), 2 mapping(s) fclear() #> All formats cleared from library.

title: ksformat 0.8.3
text: Added flevels(): extracts discrete value-label mappings ks_format object (registered format name) tidy two-column data.frame (value, label). fnew() now supports numeric pattern mode type = "numeric": pass one unnamed %f-style pattern (example "$%,.2f" "%.1f%%") format continuous numeric values directly.

title: ksformat 0.8.3
text: Added vignette Example 31: Numeric Pattern Formatting covering currency/grouping, suffix text, .missing/.handling. Added runnable companion script: examples/NumericPatterns.R.

title: ksformat 0.8.1
text: Added vignette example showing use franges() labels directly tidyr::complete() extract unique labels aesev format mappings without converting factor().

title: ksformat 0.8.1
text: R/format_parse.R split two: text--object parsing stays format_parse.R (874 lines); object--text rendering CSV CNTLOUT import/export (fexport(), fimport(), .format_to_text(), etc.) moves new R/format_serialize.R (755 lines). Three DRY internal helpers added utilities.R: .is_eval_label(), .parse_range_key_by_type(), .format_range_interval(). Duplicate switch/-chains across format_apply.R, format_create.R, format_library_app.R now delegate helpers. Range-table now carries discrete_numeric_possible flag computed format creation time. fput() / fput_all() use skip .character() + match() discrete-key pass numeric / Date / POSIXt inputs formats whose discrete keys non-numeric strings, broadening existing fast path. Friendly-interval regex .block_to_stratified_range_format() now built per format parse instead per mapping entry.

title: ksformat 0.7.2
text: finput() gains ignore_case argument. TRUE, label lookup case-insensitive (equivalent SAS INVALUE name (nocase)). Default FALSE preserves existing behaviour. fparse() now correctly propagates (nocase) option INVALUE blocks resulting ks_invalue object. Previously flag parsed silently discarded. fexport() / fparse() round-trips now preserve ignore_case invalue objects: exporting nocase invalue emits INVALUE name (nocase) re-imports ignore_case = TRUE. print.ks_invalue() now displays (nocase) flag header ignore_case = TRUE.

title: ksformat 0.7.1
text: CRAN release: 2026-05-21

title: ksformat 0.7.1
text: fputk() gains na_as_string argument. TRUE, NA key component preserved literal string "NA" produced paste() instead restored NA_character_. enables round-trip lookups formats built via fmap(paste(..., sep = "|"), values), stored keys contain "NA". Default FALSE preserves existing behavior (NA → .missing / keep_na). New finputk() — composite-label wrapper around INVALUE lookup, mirroring fputk() reverse direction. Supports sep na_as_string arguments dispatches stored invalue’s target_type.

title: ksformat 0.7.1
text: Vignette Example 28: Composite Key Lookup NA Components — explains paste()-built keys contain literal string "NA" NA inputs, shows incorrect result default na_as_string = FALSE, demonstrates correct round-trip na_as_string = TRUE using clinical LB PARAMCD derivation scenario. Vignette Example 29: Composite Label Invalue Lookup finputk() — basic two-column reverse lookup na_as_string = TRUE usage NA key components. examples/CompositeKeyNA.R — runnable companion script examples.

title: ksformat 0.7.0
text: New format type stratified_range — combines discrete stratum (study arm, subject id, composite key, …) per-stratum numeric / Date / POSIXct range buckets. Apply fputk(), passing stratum column(s) first value column last. New builder fmap_strata(stratum, low, high, label, inc_low, inc_high, sep) — produces ks_fmap ready fnew(..., type = "stratified_range"). chosen separator carried attribute picked automatically fnew(). New builder fmap_ranges(low, high, label, inc_low, inc_high) — convenience helper turns parallel vectors numeric / Date / POSIXct bounds labels canonical range keys, removing need hand-craft "low,high,inc_low,inc_high" strings. fparse() / fexport() support new type via VALUE name (stratified_range, range_subtype: numeric|date|datetime, strata_sep: |) block syntax. canonical "STRATUM|low,high,inc_low,inc_high" keys friendly "STRATUM"|[low, high) interval form accepted, including per-stratum .missing|STRATUM / .|STRATUM directives. print.ks_format() renders stratified formats grouped Stratum "X": headers.

title: ksformat 0.7.0
text: Vignette Example 26: Stratified Range Lookup fputk() — programmatic text-based construction, date subtype per-subject windows, per-stratum global .fallbacks, fexport()/fparse() roundtrip. Vignette Example 27: Plain Range Lookup fmap_ranges() — builds numeric age-band format without hand-crafted canonical keys. examples/StratifiedRanges.R — runnable companion script.

title: ksformat 0.6.7
text: Added vignette Example 25: Date Range Bucketing covering date_range datetime_range types: fiscal-year bucketing, fparse() ISO bounds, LOW/HIGH open-ended arms, fput_all() multilabel overlapping windows, auto-detection, fexport()/fparse() roundtrip, datetime_range shift bucketing. Added examples/DateRanges.R — self-contained runnable script scenarios quick interactive exploration.

title: ksformat 0.6.6
text: date_range datetime_range format types: bucket Date POSIXct values character labels using interval bounds written ISO date/datetime strings. types reuse numeric range-table fast path (sorted-disjoint ranges hit findInterval() C). fnew() accepts type = "date_range" / "datetime_range" new optional date_format argument parsing custom bound strings. fparse() accepts interval notation date/datetime bounds, e.g. [2024-01-01, 2025-01-01) = "FY24" [2024-01-01 08:00, 2024-01-01 16:00) = "Day". type auto-detected explicit subtype given. fexport() renders date/datetime range bounds ISO strings, enabling full roundtrip fparse(). Bounds support LOW / HIGH keywords (rendered -Inf / +Inf emitted back LOW / HIGH export) exclusive ( / ) brackets.

title: ksformat 0.6.5
text: Precomputed range table: ks_format objects now carry pre-built range_table field. Range keys parsed format-creation time (fnew(), fparse(), fimport()) rather every fput() call. findInterval() fast path fput(): sorted, non-overlapping numeric ranges standard [low, high) semantics now use R’s built-findInterval() (O(n log k) C), giving ~10–14× speedup previous per-range R loop large inputs (benchmarked 1M rows). skip_discrete optimisation fput(): pure numeric-range formats (discrete keys) numeric input now skip .character() + match() step entirely. is_missing(): removed redundant .nan() pass numeric vectors — .na() already returns TRUE NaN.

title: ksformat 0.6.5
text: .build_range_table(): ranges defined --order now sorted (low, high) storage, findInterval fast path triggers regardless definition order. .build_range_table(): removed dead attr<- loop silently effect (subsetting character vector creates copy, stripping attribute set subset).

title: ksformat 0.6.4
text: Added franges(): extracts range entries ks_format object tidy data.frame columns low, high, inc_low, inc_high, label. Accepts either ks_format object registered format name. Added fmap_to_ranges(): reverse-looks range bounds label — given vector coded values match range labels format, returns corresponding low / high bounds (inclusivity flags) per element.

title: ksformat 0.6.3
text: Fixed fput() (fputk()) losing tzone attribute returning POSIXct values value-type format. result vector seeded tzone-less NA, values silently displayed local timezone instead source timezone (e.g. UTC), causing apparent time-shift. tzone attribute mapped values now propagated result.

title: ksformat 0.6.2

title: ksformat 0.6.2
text: Replaced plain-text print preview app formatted mapping table shows mappings, range rules, special directives (.missing, .) structured form. Added app management UX improvements: explicit quit action confirmation modal, plus confirmations destructive clear/remove operations.

title: ksformat 0.6.2

Vignette Example 22 added: “Date Lookup via fparse() fputk()” — character lookup, native Date lookup fmap(), round-trip via fexport()/fparse().

title: ksformat 0.6.0

Set reverse = FALSE use natural setNames(values, keys) convention consistently format types — character, numeric, Date, POSIXct, logical. default (NULL) preserves backward-compatible auto-detection: reversal character/numeric, reversal value types.
